tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    it_security_nodes IT security (data leak) fault tree nodes dataset

    it_security_nodes.Rd

    An example dataset of nodes in a fault tree for sensitive data leaving the organization. The tree models three ways a breach can happen: an outsider gets high-level access, an attacker uses a known flaw that was not fixed, or someone inside sends data out.

    it_security_nodes

    Format

    it_security_nodes

    A data frame with 16 rows and 3 columns:

    id

    Unique identifier (1 to 16) for each node.

    event

    Two-letter event code. "DE" = Sensitive data leaves the organization (top). Gates: "BP" = Ways a breach can happen (OR), "EA" = Outsider gets high-level access (AND), "UE" = Attacker uses a known flaw that was not fixed (AND), "IE" = Someone inside sends data out (AND), "CC" = Log-in details stolen or misused (OR). Basic events: "PC" = Passwords obtained via fake email or link, "LR" = Passwords exposed in a breach or reused elsewhere, "MN" = No second login step required, "PM" = Admin-level access set up incorrectly, "VS" = System has a known security hole, "PO" = Security updates not applied on time, "WB" = Web traffic filter missing or bypassed, "IM" = Employee did it on purpose or account was taken over, "DA" = No controls to stop data from being copied out, "EP" = User had more access than needed for their job.

    type

    Factor classification as "top", "and", "or", or "not".

    Details

    The fault tree structure (event codes):

    • Top event: DE (Sensitive data leaves the organization)

    • BP (OR): EA OR UE OR IE

    • EA (AND): CC AND MN AND PM

    • UE (AND): VS AND PO AND WB

    • IE (AND): IM AND DA AND EP

    • CC (OR): PC OR LR

    See also

    it_security_edges for the corresponding edges dataset

    Examples

    data("it_security_nodes")
head(it_security_nodes)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>      id event type 
#>   <dbl> <chr> <fct>
#> 1     1 DE    top  
#> 2     2 BP    or   
#> 3     3 EA    and  
#> 4     4 UE    and  
#> 5     5 IE    and  
#> 6     6 CC    or   
dim(it_security_nodes)
#> [1] 16  3

    Contents
